subject: Stefánia út 107, Budapest XIV district — Swiss Embassy building, 1943
context_for_lesson: A 1943 photograph of Stefánia út in Budapest's 14th district — the same Budapest street where Judy Abrams's family lived before going into hiding. This specific image shows #107, the wartime Swiss Embassy. The Swiss legation in Budapest, led by diplomat Carl Lutz, became a key refuge issuing protective letters that saved tens of thousands of Hungarian Jews in 1944. While Judy's family lived at a different address on the same street, this photograph provides authentic period imagery of the Stefánia út streetscape Judy would have known as a child.
publisher_or_host: Wikimedia Commons (originally from Fortepan archive / Archiv fur Zeitgeschichte ETH Zurich / Agnes Hirschi)
author: FOTO:FORTEPAN / Archiv fur Zeitgeschichte ETH Zurich / Agnes Hirschi

attribution_required: Photo: FORTEPAN / Archiv fur Zeitgeschichte ETH Zurich / Agnes Hirschi via Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 3.0)
lesson_use_intent: Page that introduces Judy's pre-war life in Budapest, or page that describes her family's flight from Stefánia út. Caption MUST clarify: 'Stefánia út, Budapest, 1943. The street where Judy lived as a young child. This building, the Swiss Embassy, would soon issue protective letters saving thousands of Hungarian Jews. (Judy's family lived at a different address on this same street.)' Do NOT imply this is Judy's specific home.
concerns: Caption must clearly state this is Stefánia út generally, NOT Judy's specific apartment. Do not crop in such a way that a student could mistake the building for Judy's home.
